description_long: This house features a steeply pitched gable roof with a prominent triangular peak at the front, evoking a classic Victorian or Gothic Revival style. The facade is painted in a warm ochre color, accented by gray window frames and roof elements. The front elevation includes a large arched window above the main entrance, a rectangular window to the side, and a covered porch area with a flat roof extension. The side and rear views reveal a more utilitarian design, with fewer windows and a focus on solid wall surfaces. The roof is primarily gabled, with a secondary flat-roofed section over the porch. The overall structure is compact, two stories tall, and likely constructed from masonry or wood with a stucco or painted exterior finish.
description: A two-story ochre house with a steep gable roof, arched and rectangular windows, and a covered front porch.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entrance, large arched window, rectangular window, and covered porch with a flat roof.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the solid ochre wall, flat-roofed porch extension, and minimal window openings.
description_view_2: Rear view dominated by a plain ochre wall and the sharp peak of the gable roof, with no visible windows or doors.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with a solid ochre wall and the flat-roofed porch extension visible, similar to view 1 but mirrored.
attribute_window_arrangement: asymmetrical placement with arched and rectangular windows
attribute_entryway_design: Main entryway with a rectangular door, covered by a flat-roofed porch extension, and accented by a large arched window above.
